name: Skypeia
description: Skypeia is a legendary sky island located 10,000 meters above the sea, accessible via the Knock Up Stream. It's a mystical realm where clouds form the ground and the sea, and ancient secrets of the Void Century lie hidden.
history: Skypeia was originally part of Jaya island before being shot into the sky 400 years ago by the Knock Up Stream. The Shandians, the island's original inhabitants, were brought up with it and have been fighting to reclaim their homeland from the Skypieans ever since. The island contains ancient ruins and a Poneglyph inscribed by the Pirate King Gol D. Roger. Enel, a Logia-type Devil Fruit user, took control of Skypeia and ruled as a tyrannical 'God' until he was defeated by Monkey D. Luffy.
culture: Skypiean culture revolves around the worship of 'God' (their leader), cloud-based architecture, and the concept of 'Vearth' (earth/soil) being incredibly precious. They use dials - special shells that can store various forms of energy. The Shandians maintain their warrior culture and light the 'Light of Shandora' as a beacon for their ancestors. Music and the sound of the Golden Bell play significant roles in their traditions.
transportation: Citizens travel using Wavers (motorized cloud surfboards powered by Breath Dials), Sky Fish riding, and special cloud paths called Milky Road. The White-White Sea and White Sea serve as cloud oceans for navigation.

quirks: Inhabitants are shocked by the concept of dirt
Sea dwellers are treated as exotic

notablePeople.name: Enel
notablePeople.description: A Logia-type Devil Fruit user with the Goro Goro no Mi (Rumble-Rumble Fruit), giving him lightning powers. He ruled Skypeia with an iron fist until defeated by Luffy.

notablePeople.name: Gan Fall
notablePeople.role: Former God of Skypeia / Knight of the Sky
notablePeople.description: The benevolent former ruler of Skypeia who was overthrown by Enel. He rides a giant bird named Pierre and fights to protect his people.

notablePeople.name: Wyper
notablePeople.description: A fierce descendant of Calgara who leads the Shandians in their 400-year war to reclaim their homeland. Wields the Burn Bazooka and Reject Dial.

notablePeople.name: Conis
notablePeople.description: A kind Skypiean girl who befriends the Straw Hat Pirates and helps them understand Sky Island culture. Her father Pagaya is an engineer.

notablePeople.name: Pagaya
notablePeople.description: Conis's father, a skilled engineer who builds Wavers and understands Dial technology. Helps the Straw Hats navigate Sky Island culture.

notablePeople.name: Ohm
notablePeople.description: One of Enel's four priests who guards the Ordeal of Iron. Uses Iron Cloud and wields a massive sword. Known for his nihilistic philosophy.

notablePeople.name: Satori
notablePeople.description: One of Enel's priests with powerful Mantra abilities. Guards the Ordeal of Balls and fights using Surprise Balls filled with various weapons.

notablePeople.name: Gedatsu
notablePeople.description: A comically absent-minded priest who guards the Ordeal of Swamp. Despite his scattered personality, he's a formidable fighter with Jet Dial boots.

notablePeople.name: Aisa
notablePeople.description: A young Shandian girl born with natural Mantra (Observation Haki) abilities. Can sense the presence and emotions of others across great distances.

notablePeople.name: Calgara
notablePeople.description: The great warrior of Shandia 400 years ago who befriended the explorer Noland. His friendship and the promise to ring the Golden Bell became legend.

notablePeople.name: Montblanc Noland
notablePeople.description: The legendary explorer who visited Jaya 400 years ago and became friends with Calgara. Known as 'Noland the Liar' in North Blue tales, but his story was true.

subLocations.name: Upper Yard
subLocations.description: The original landmass of Jaya that was shot into the sky 400 years ago. Contains ancient ruins, the Golden City of Shandora, and the legendary Golden Bell.

subLocations.name: Angel Island
subLocations.description: The main Skypiean settlement with residential areas, Lovely Street shopping district, and cloud harbors.

subLocations.name: God's Shrine
subLocations.description: Enel's base of operations, featuring the Arc Maxim construction site and throne room. Located in the heart of Upper Yard.

subLocations.name: Cloud End
subLocations.description: The edge of the White-White Sea where the clouds end, revealing the vast ocean 10,000 meters below.

subLocations.name: Shandora Ruins
subLocations.description: The ruins of the ancient golden city built by the Shandians. Contains valuable archaeological treasures and the Poneglyph.

items.name: Flame Dial
items.description: Stores and releases fire for offensive attacks. Can be used as a weapon or for cooking.

items.name: Impact Dial
items.description: Absorbs and releases impact force. Used for powerful physical attacks but damages the user's body.

items.name: Reject Dial
items.description: The ultimate Impact Dial that releases 10 times the absorbed impact. Extremely dangerous to the user.

items.name: Tone Dial
items.description: Records and plays back sound. Used for music, communication, and entertainment.

items.name: Vision Dial
items.description: Records and plays back images like a camera. Can store visual memories.

items.name: Breath Dial
items.description: Stores and releases wind. Powers Wavers and provides propulsion.

items.name: Jet Dial
items.description: A more powerful version of Breath Dial. Provides explosive speed boosts.

items.name: Milky Dial
items.description: Stores and releases clouds for creating Milky Roads and cloud paths.

items.name: Water Dial
items.description: Stores and releases water. Useful for drinking and extinguishing fires.

items.name: Axe Dial
items.description: Releases a blade of compressed air that can cut through objects.

factions.name: Enel's Priests
factions.description: Enel's enforcers who maintain his tyrannical rule over Skypeia through fear and the Ordeal system. Each priest guards a different trial.

factions.name: Shandian Warriors
factions.description: Descendants of the original inhabitants of Jaya, fighting for 400 years to reclaim their homeland and light the Golden Bell.

factions.name: Skypieans
factions.description: The peaceful cloud-dwelling citizens of Angel Island who were caught between Enel's tyranny and the Shandian war.

quickFacts: Home to the legendary Golden Bell of Shandora
Contains a Poneglyph with ancient history
Connected to the Void Century mysteries

videos.title: Luffy Rings the Golden Bell
videos.description: The emotional climax where Luffy rings the bell, fulfilling a 400-year promise.

videos.title: Luffy vs Enel - Final Battle
videos.description: Epic battle between the rubber man and the lightning god.
